amendments.version_after_text: The following public institutions of the state are permanently located at the places hereinafter named, each to have the lands specifically granted to it by the United States in the Act of Congress approved February 22, 1889, to be disposed of and used in such manner as the legislative assembly may prescribe subject to the limitations provided in the article on school and public lands contained in this constitution. 1. The seat of government at the city of Bismarck in the county of Burleigh. 2. The state university and the school of mines at the city of Grand Forks, in the county of Grand Forks. 3. The North Dakota state university of agriculture and applied science at the city of Fargo, in the county of Cass. 4. A state normal school at the city of Valley City, in the county of Barnes, and the legislative assembly, in apportioning the grant of eighty thousand acres of land for normal schools made in the Act of Congress referred to shall grant to the said normal school at Valley City, as aforementioned, fifty thousand (50,000) acres, and said lands are hereby appropriated to said institution for that purpose. 5. The school for the deaf and hard of hearing at the city of Devils Lake, in the county of Ramsey. 6. A state training school at the city of Mandan, in the county of Morton. 7. A state normal school at the city of Mayville, in the county of Traill, and the legislative assembly in apportioning the grant of lands made by Congress in the Act aforesaid for state normal schools shall assign thirty thousand (30,000) acres to the institution hereby located at Mayville, and said lands are hereby appropriated for said purpose. 8. A state hospital for the care of individuals with mental illness at the city of Jamestown, in the county of Stutsman. And the legislative assembly shall appropriate twenty thousand acres of the grant of lands made by the Act of Congress aforesaid for other educational and charitable institutions to the benefit and for the endowment of said institution, and there shall be located at or near the city of Grafton, in the county of Walsh, a facility for individuals with developmental disabilities, on the grounds purchased by the secretary of the interior for a penitentiary building.
amendments.version_before_text: The following public institutions of the state are permanently located at the places hereinafter named, each to have the lands specifically granted to it by the United States in the Act of Congress approved February 22, 1889, to be disposed of and used in such manner as the legislative assembly may prescribe subject to the limitations provided in the article on school and public lands contained in this constitution. 1. The seat of government at the city of Bismarck in the county of Burleigh. 2. The state university and the school of mines at the city of Grand Forks, in the county of Grand Forks. 3. The North Dakota state university of agriculture and applied science at the city of Fargo, in the county of Cass. 4. A state normal school at the city of Valley City, in the county of Barnes, and the legislative assembly, in apportioning the grant of eighty thousand acres of land for normal schools made in the Act of Congress referred to shall grant to the said normal school at Valley City, as aforementioned, fifty thousand (50,000) acres, and said lands are hereby appropriated to said institution for that purpose. 5. The school for the deaf and dumb of North Dakota at the city of Devils Lake, in the county of Ramsey. 6. A state training school at the city of Mandan, in the county of Morton. 7. A state normal school at the city of Mayville, in the county of Traill, and the legislative assembly in apportioning the grant of lands made by Congress in the Act aforesaid for state normal schools shall assign thirty thousand (30,000) acres to the institution hereby located at Mayville, and said lands are hereby appropriated for said purpose. 8. A state hospital for the insane at the city of Jamestown, in the county of Stutsman. And the legislative assembly shall appropriate twenty thousand acres of the grant of lands made by the Act of Congress aforesaid for other educational and charitable institutions to the benefit and for the endowment of said institution, and there shall be located at or near the city of Grafton, in the county of Walsh, an institution for the feebleminded, on the grounds purchased by the secretary of the interior for a penitentiary building.
reverse_edit_list: deaf and hard of hearing
deaf and dumb of North Dakota
the care of individuals with mental illness at the city of Jamestown
the insane at the city of Jamestown
a facility for individuals with developmental disabilities
an institution for the feebleminded
amendments.notes: 2024 SCR 4001 'Updated Terminology' redline (bill 23-3015, p.1-2). Three changes in this section: item 5 struck 'dumb of North Dakota' / added 'hard of hearing'; item 8 struck 'insane' / added 'care of individuals with mental illness'; Grafton clause struck 'an institution' -> added 'a facility' and struck 'the feebleminded' -> added 'individuals with developmental disabilities'. IMPORTANT DISCREPANCY: the provided current_text (and the ndconst.org snapshot in the const DB, stamped effective_start 2024-12-05) still shows the PRE-amendment terminology ('deaf and dumb of North Dakota', 'insane', 'an institution for the feebleminded'). Amendment 166 was approved at the 2024-11-05 general election and is in force; the ndconst.org current-text snapshot was never updated to apply it, so version_after_text here (the true current text) does NOT match the supplied current_text. version_before_text equals the supplied current_text.

amendments.version_after_text: The following public institutions are located as provided, each to have so much of the remaining grant of one hundred seventy thousand acres of land made by the United States for "other educational and charitable institutions" as is allotted by law: 1. A soldiers' home, when located, or such other charitable institution as the legislative assembly may determine, at the city of Lisbon in the county of Ransom, with a grant of forty thousand acres of land. 2. The school for the blind at the city of Grand Forks in the county of Grand Forks or at such other location as may be determined by the legislative assembly to be in the best interests of the students of such institution and the state of North Dakota. 3. A school of forestry, or such other institution as the legislative assembly may determine, at such place in one of the counties of McHenry, Ward, Bottineau, or Rolette, as the electors of said counties may determine by an election for that purpose, to be held as provided by the legislative assembly. 4. A school of science or such other educational or charitable institution as the legislative assembly may prescribe, at the city of Wahpeton in the county of Richland, with a grant of forty thousand acres. 5. A state college at the city of Minot in the county of Ward. 6. A state college at the city of Dickinson in the county of Stark. 7. A state hospital for the mentally ill at such place within this state as shall be selected by the legislative assembly. No other institution of a character similar to any one of those located by article IX, section 12, or this section shall be established or maintained without an amendment of this constitution.
amendments.version_before_text: The following named public institutions are hereby permanently located as hereinafter provided, each to have so much of the remaining grant of one hundred seventy thousand acres of land made by the United States for "other educational and charitable institutions" as is allotted by law, namely: 1. A soldiers' home, when located, or such other charitable institution as the legislative assembly may determine, at Lisbon, in the county of Ransom, with a grant of forty thousand acres of land. 2. The blind asylum shall be known as the North Dakota school for the blind and may be removed from the county of Pembina to such other location as may be determined by the board of administration to be in the best interests of the students of such institution and the state of North Dakota. 3. A school of forestry, or such other institution as the legislative assembly may determine, at such place in one of the counties of McHenry, Ward, Bottineau, or Rolette, as the electors of said counties may determine by an election for that purpose, to be held as provided by the legislative assembly. 4. A scientific school or such other educational or charitable institution as the legislative assembly may prescribe, at the city of Wahpeton, county of Richland, with a grant of forty thousand acres. 5. A state normal school at the city of Minot in the county of Ward. 6. a. A state normal school at the city of Dickinson, in the county of Stark. b. A state hospital for the insane at such place within this state as shall be selected by the legislative assembly, provided, that no other institution of a character similar to any one of those located by this article shall be established or maintained without a revision of this constitution.

amendments.notes: 1982 amendment 114 'Obsolete Constitutional References' (1983 S.L. ch. 722, SECTION 3, reprint of 'Section 13 of article IX' spanning CAA pp. 2235-2237 / PDF pp. 9-10). Redline changes: intro struck 'named', 'hereby permanently', 'hereinafter', and trailing ', namely'; item 1 added 'the city of' before Lisbon and struck the comma after Lisbon; item 2 struck 'blind asylum shall be known as the North Dakota' (before 'school'), struck 'and may be removed from the county of Pembina to' (after 'for the blind'), added 'at the city of Grand Forks in the county of Grand Forks or at' (after 'for the blind'), and struck 'board of administration' -> added 'legislative assembly'; item 4 struck 'scientific' before 'school' / added 'of science' after, and 'at the city of Wahpeton,' struck comma / added 'in the' before 'county of Richland'; item 5 struck 'normal school' -> added 'college'; old item-6 sub-letter 'a.' struck and item renumbered to 6 with 'normal school' -> 'college' and comma after Dickinson struck; old item 6 sub-letter 'b.' struck and renumbered to new item 7 with 'insane' -> 'mentally ill' and trailing ', provided, that no' struck; final standalone sentence formed by adding capital 'No', adding 'article IX, section 12, or', struck 'article' -> added 'section', and struck 'a revision' -> added 'an amendment'. version_after_text matches the supplied current_text exactly (continuity with amendment 166's version_before).

amendments.notes: 2024 SCR 4001 'Updated Terminology' redline (bill 23-3015, p.2). Single change in this section: item 7 struck 'mentally ill' / added 'care of individuals with mental illness'. The final sentence ('No other institution...') had NO redline. DISCREPANCY: provided current_text (and ndconst.org snapshot, stamped effective_start 2024-12-05) still shows the pre-amendment 'mentally ill'; amendment 166 is in force (approved 2024-11-05 general election) but the snapshot was never updated. version_after_text here is the true current text; version_before_text equals the supplied current_text.
